learning_outcomes.description: Explore advanced epidemiological concepts, study designs and statistical methods.
Understand the principles and techniques of causal inference in epidemiological research.
Critically evaluate epidemiological literature and research studies with a focus on causal inference.
Apply advanced epidemiological and causal inference methods to real-world research questions and datasets.
Demonstrate effective skills in communication for a professional audience.

assessments.task_description: This assessment will involve performing analyses to examine a causal research question using methods covered in the module. The report will require students to consider appropriate presentation of their data analyses and results, as well as answer questions related to the interpretation of results and a critical evaluation.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assessments should be submitted as a Word document via Turnitin on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Late assessments will not be accepted without prior approval. If an extension is required, you must apply online via my.UQ (see the SPH policy on late submissions and extensions).
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Applied GIS and spatial analysis using real world data
assessments.task_description: Students will be provided with a spatially indexed epidemiological dataset and asked to analyse the data using appropriate spatial epidemiological and GIS methods covered in the module. They must present results clearly and interpret them in a public health context, aligning with the module’s intended learning outcomes.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assessments should be submitted as a Word document via Turnitin on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Late assessments will not be accepted without prior approval. If an extension is required, you must apply online via my.UQ (see the SPH policy on late submissions and extensions).
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
